figures.caption: Figure 4: Ablation study of mask ratio m and hyperparameter ν when trained with our proposed reward function (R = RREP + λRPTRIM) after self-supervised pretraining on TVSUM [41] dataset. Each correlation type is visually distinguished by a unique combination of color (hue), line style, and marker.

Figure 3: Ablation study of mask ratio m and hyperparameter ν when trained with our proposed reward function (R = RREP +λRPTRIM) after self-supervised pretraining on SUMME [40] dataset. Each correlation type is visually distinguished by a unique combination of color (hue), line style, and marker.
Figure 1: Overview of the proposed two-stage TRIMMER method. The first stage is indicated using orange and brown arrows while the second reinforcement learning stage is indicated using purple arrows.

Figure 7: Ablation study results from Table 2, illustrating the contribution of each reward function within our two-stage TRIMMER framework. Performance is evaluated by comparing predicted importance scores to ground-truth annotations (averaged over 20 annotators) for the video "xwqBXPGE9pQ" from the TVSum dataset [41].

Table 1: Comparative evaluation of the proposed framework TRIMMER against existing state-of-theart methods using ranking correlation metrics—Spearman’s ρ and Kendall’s τ . Methods categorized as unsupervised or self-supervised are shaded in gray. The columns SUP and SSL denote whether a method employs supervised or self-supervised learning, respectively; FT indicates if the network has been fine-tuned after self-supervision; ATTN specifies the use of attention mechanisms in the network; and GFLOPs reports the computational complexity measured in gigaflops.

Figure 5: Ablation study showing the progression of mean rewards across all videos during training, evaluated via five-fold cross-validation using a fixed random seed. The comparison includes individual reward components RPTRIM, RREP, and the cumulative reward defined as R = RPTRIM + λRREP, used during the reinforcement learning phase (Stage 2) following pretraining on the TVSUM dataset [41] (Figure 5a) and SUMME dataset [40] (Figure 5b). For improved visual clarity, reward values were scaled to facilitate alignment across curves.

Figure 6: Ablation study results from Table 2, illustrating the contribution of each reward function within our proposed two-stage TRIMMER framework. Performance is evaluated by comparing predicted importance scores to ground-truth annotations (averaged over 20 annotators) for the video "eQu1rNs0an0" from the TVSum dataset [41].
Table 3: Comparison of computational complexity (in terms of Big-O notation) for each reward function component in TRIMMER with TRIM, and DRDSN [6].
Figure 2: Ablation study on the influence of hyperparameter λ trained with our proposed reward (R = RREP + λRPTRIM) function after self-supervision pre-training on TVSUM[41] and SUMME[40] datasets.
